caseStudy.title: Real-Time Analytics at Scale
caseStudy.description: Powering sub-second analytics over petabyte-scale datasets.
caseStudy.overview: A digital advertising platform needed to provide advertisers with real-time campaign performance dashboards over datasets exceeding 10 petabytes. Existing query infrastructure could not meet sub-second latency requirements at the required concurrency levels.
caseStudy.problem: Advertiser-facing dashboards refreshed every 15 minutes and timed out under concurrent load. Campaigns could run for hours before advertisers detected performance issues, resulting in significant wasted spend and eroded platform trust.
caseStudy.solution: The query architecture was redesigned around a cloud data warehouse with aggressive materialised view strategies, result caching, and workload isolation for advertiser queries. Data ingestion was refactored to update materialised views incrementally in near-real-time as new event data arrived.

results.summary: Advertisers now access campaign performance data with seconds of latency, enabling real-time budget adjustments that improve campaign ROI. Platform NPS improved significantly following the launch.
